content:
<EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>
You have superpowers.

**Below is the full content of your 'superpowers:using-superpowers' skill - your introduction to using skills. For all other skills, use the 'Skill' tool:**

---
name: using-superpowers
description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
---

<SUBAGENT-STOP>
If you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific task, skip this skill.
</SUBAGENT-STOP>

<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>
If you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply to what you are doing, you ABSOLUTELY MUST invoke the skill.

IF A SKILL APPLIES TO YOUR TASK, YOU DO NOT HAVE A CHOICE. YOU MUST USE IT.

This is not negotiable. This is not optional. You cannot rationalize your way out of this.
</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>

## Instruction Priority

Superpowers skills override default system prompt behavior, but **user instructions always take precedence**:

1. **User's explicit instructions** (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md, direct requests) — highest priority
2. **Superpowers skills** — override default system behavior where they conflict
3. **Default system prompt** — lowest priority

If CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, or AGENTS.md says "don't use TDD" and a skill says "always use TDD," follow the user's instructions. The user is in control.

## How to Access Skills

**In Claude Code:** Use the `Skill` tool. When you invoke a skill, its content is loaded and presented to you—follow it directly. Never use the Read tool on skill files.

**In Copilot CLI:** Use the `skill` tool. Skills are auto-discovered from installed plugins. The `skill` tool works the same as Claude Code's `Skill` tool.

**In Gemini CLI:** Skills activate via the `activate_skill` tool. Gemini loads skill metadata at session start and activates the full content on demand.

**In other environments:** Check your platform's documentation for how skills are loaded.

## Platform Adaptation

Skills use Claude Code tool names. Non-CC platforms: see `references/copilot-tools.md` (Copilot CLI), `references/codex-tools.md` (Codex) for tool equivalents. Gemini CLI users get the tool mapping loaded automatically via GEMINI.md.

# Using Skills

## The Rule

**Invoke relevant or requested skills BEFORE any response or action.** Even a 1% chance a skill might apply means that you should invoke the skill to check. If an invoked skill turns out to be wrong for the situation, you don't need to use it.

```dot
digraph skill_flow {
    "User message received" [shape=doublecircle];
    "About to EnterPlanMode?" [shape=doublecircle];
    "Already brainstormed?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" [shape=box];
    "Might any skill apply?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke Skill tool" [shape=box];
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" [shape=box];
    "Has checklist?" [shape=diamond];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [shape=box];
    "Follow skill exactly" [shape=box];
    "Respond (including clarifications)" [shape=doublecircle];

    "About to EnterPlanMode?" -> "Already brainstormed?";
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Invoke brainstorming skill" [label="no"];
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Might any skill apply?" [label="yes"];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" -> "Might any skill apply?";

    "User message received" -> "Might any skill apply?";
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Invoke Skill tool" [label="yes, even 1%"];
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Respond (including clarifications)" [label="definitely not"];
    "Invoke Skill tool" -> "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'";
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" -> "Has checklist?";
    "Has checklist?" -> "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [label="yes"];
    "Has checklist?" -> "Follow skill exactly" [label="no"];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" -> "Follow skill exactly";
}
```

## Red Flags

These thoughts mean STOP—you're rationalizing:

| Thought | Reality |
|---------|---------|
| "This is just a simple question" | Questions are tasks. Check for skills. |
| "I need more context first" | Skill check comes BEFORE clarifying questions. |
| "Let me explore the codebase first" | Skills tell you HOW to explore. Check first. |
| "I can check git/files quickly" | Files lack conversation context. Check for skills. |
| "Let me gather information first" | Skills tell you HOW to gather information. |
| "This doesn't need a formal skill" | If a skill exists, use it. |
| "I remember this skill" | Skills evolve. Read current version. |
| "This doesn't count as a task" | Action = task. Check for skills. |
| "The skill is overkill" | Simple things become complex. Use it. |
| "I'll just do this one thing first" | Check BEFORE doing anything. |
| "This feels productive" | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this. |
| "I know what that means" | Knowing the concept ≠ using the skill. Invoke it. |

## Skill Priority

When multiple skills could apply, use this order:

1. **Process skills first** (brainstorming, debugging) - these determine HOW to approach the task
2. **Implementation skills second** (frontend-design, mcp-builder) - these guide execution

"Let's build X" → brainstorming first, then implementation skills.
"Fix this bug" → debugging first, then domain-specific skills.

## Skill Types

**Rigid** (TDD, debugging): Follow exactly. Don't adapt away discipline.

**Flexible** (patterns): Adapt principles to context.

The skill itself tells you which.

## User Instructions

Instructions say WHAT, not HOW. "Add X" or "Fix Y" doesn't mean skip workflows.


</EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>

## claude.ai Linear
When passing string values to tools, send the content directly without escape sequences. For example, use real newlines in markdown content rather than literal backslash-n (\n) characters.

## plugin:context7-plugin:context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

## plugin:context7:context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

- brainstorming: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.
- dispatching-parallel-agents: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies
- executing-plans: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- finishing-a-development-branch: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup
- playwright-cli: Automates browser interactions for web testing, form filling, screenshots, and data extraction. Use when the user needs to navigate websites, interact with web pages, fill forms, take screenshots, test web applications, or extract information from web pages.
- receiving-code-review: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation
- requesting-code-review: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements
- subagent-driven-development: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session
- systematic-debugging: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes
- test-driven-development: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code
- using-git-worktrees: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - creates isolated git worktrees with smart directory selection and safety verification
- using-superpowers: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
- verification-before-completion: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
- writing-plans: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code
- writing-skills: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment
- dioxus: Dioxus 0.7 reactive UI framework reference (covers dioxus, dioxus-hooks, dioxus-signals, dioxus-stores, dioxus-router, dioxus-fullstack, dioxus-document). Use when writing Dioxus components, signals, effects, resources, server functions, stores, routing, rsx macro code, or fullstack/SSR apps. Also use when the user mentions Dioxus, asks about reactive patterns in Dioxus, or works on any .rs file that imports from dioxus::prelude::*. Even if the question seems simple, consult this skill — Dioxus APIs change frequently between versions and training data is often wrong.
- icu4x: ICU4X 2.x internationalization crate family reference (covers icu_calendar, icu_datetime, icu_plurals, icu_decimal, icu_collator, icu_segmenter, icu_locale, icu_provider, icu_experimental, fixed_decimal). Use when writing or reviewing any Rust code that uses ICU4X crates for i18n, l10n, date/time formatting, number formatting, plural rules, collation, text segmentation, or locale handling. Also use when the user mentions ICU4X, works on any .rs file that imports from icu::* or icu_*::*, or reviews spec files referencing ICU4X APIs. Even if the question seems simple, consult this skill — ICU4X had a major breaking redesign from 1.x to 2.x and training data is almost certainly wrong.
- leptos: Leptos 0.8 reactive web framework reference (covers leptos, leptos_router, reactive_stores, server_fn, leptos_meta). Use when writing Leptos components, signals, effects, resources, actions, server functions, stores, routing, view macro code, or SSR/hydration. Also use when the user mentions Leptos, asks about reactive patterns in Leptos, or works on any .rs file that imports from leptos::*. Even if the question seems simple, consult this skill — Leptos APIs change frequently and training data is often wrong.
- playwright-cli: Automates browser interactions for web testing, form filling, screenshots, and data extraction. Use when the user needs to navigate websites, interact with web pages, fill forms, take screenshots, test web applications, or extract information from web pages.
- post-implementation-audit: MANDATORY after every implementation task — runs three sequential audits on the new code (spec/impl drift, iterative "anything missing?", test coverage) and lands every finding in the same PR before the user-review step. Invoke as soon as an implementation task's named tests pass, OR when any of these phrases appears: "ready to present", "implementation is complete", "named tests pass", "before opening the PR", "ready for review". Skipping this audit leaves spec drift, untested defensive code, missing conventions, and silent contract violations in the merged PR.
- waiting-for-codex-review: Use immediately after pushing a PR to GitHub when Codex is configured to review the branch. Codex reviews asynchronously — it leaves a 👀 reaction on the PR while reviewing, drops the 👀 when the run finishes (either replacing it with 👍 if satisfied, or removing it and posting review threads if changes are needed). This skill defines the polling loop and the response workflow: wait for Codex's verdict, address any review threads it posts with the same rigor as `post-implementation-audit` (TDD + no coverage regression), commit, push, reply inline, resolve the thread, then keep waiting until 👍. Triggers on phrases like "wait for codex review", "what does codex say", "is codex done", "address the codex feedback", "check the PR reviews", or any post-push handoff where the user expects an automated reviewer's verdict. Invoke this skill even when the user does not explicitly name Codex — any GitHub PR where a 👀/👍 reaction signals an automated reviewer follows the same pattern.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.

- rust-skills:rust-skill-creator: Use when creating skills for Rust crates or std library documentation. Keywords: create rust skill, create crate skill, create std skill, 创建 rust skill, 创建 crate skill, 创建 std skill, 动态 rust skill, 动态 crate skill, skill for tokio, skill for serde, skill for axum, generate rust skill, rust 技能, crate 技能, 从文档创建skill, from docs create skill
- rust-skills:rust-symbol-analyzer: Analyze Rust project structure using LSP symbols. Triggers on: /symbols, project structure, list structs, list traits, list functions, 符号分析, 项目结构, 列出所有, 有哪些struct
- rust-skills:rust-trait-explorer: Explore Rust trait implementations using LSP. Triggers on: /trait-impl, find implementations, who implements, trait 实现, 谁实现了, 实现了哪些trait
- rust-skills:unsafe-checker: CRITICAL: Use for unsafe Rust code review and FFI. Triggers on: unsafe, raw pointer, FFI, extern, transmute, *mut, *const, union, #[repr(C)], libc, std::ffi, MaybeUninit, NonNull, SAFETY comment, soundness, undefined behavior, UB, safe wrapper, memory layout, bindgen, cbindgen, CString, CStr, 安全抽象, 裸指针, 外部函数接口, 内存布局, 不安全代码, FFI 绑定, 未定义行为
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

=== RUST SKILLS DISPLAY FORMAT ===
When showing Rust Skills loaded, display in this EXACT order:
1. FIRST: "🦀 Rust Skills Loaded" text
2. THEN: The Ferris crab ASCII art BELOW the text
The text must be ABOVE the crab, not below.
===

=== MANDATORY: META-COGNITION ROUTING ===

CRITICAL: You MUST follow the COMPLETE meta-cognition framework.
Partial compliance (only loading L1 skill) is NOT acceptable.

## STEP 1: IDENTIFY ENTRY LAYER + DOMAIN

### Layer 1 Signals (Start here, trace UP):
- Error codes: E0382, E0597, E0277, E0499, etc.
- Keywords: cannot be sent, moved value, borrowed, lifetime

### Layer 3 Domain Signals (MUST also load domain skill):

| Keywords in Question | Domain Skill to Load |
|---------------------|---------------------|
| Web API, HTTP, REST, axum, actix, handler, router | domain-web |
| payment, trading, fintech, decimal, currency | domain-fintech |
| CLI, command line, clap, terminal | domain-cli |
| embedded, no_std, MCU, firmware | domain-embedded |
| kubernetes, docker, grpc, microservice | domain-cloud-native |
| MQTT, sensor, IoT, telemetry | domain-iot |
| tensor, model, inference, ML | domain-ml |

**CRITICAL**: If domain keywords are present, you MUST load BOTH:
1. The Layer 1 skill (e.g., m07-concurrency)
2. The Layer 3 domain skill (e.g., domain-web)

## STEP 2: EXECUTE TRACING (MANDATORY)

For Layer 1 entry with domain context:

```
L1 Error (e.g., Rc not Send)
    ^ TRACE UP: Why this design constraint?
    ^ Load domain skill (e.g., domain-web)
    ^ Find domain constraint (e.g., "handlers run on any thread")
    v TRACE DOWN: What pattern satisfies constraint?
    v Return to L1 with context-aware solution
```

## STEP 3: MANDATORY OUTPUT FORMAT

Your response MUST include ALL of these sections:

### Reasoning Chain
```
+-- Layer 1: [specific error]
|   Problem: [error description]
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: [domain name] (domain-xxx)
|   Constraint: [what is the domain constraint]
|   Rule: [why this constraint exists]
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: [design decision based on domain constraint]
```

### Domain Constraints Analysis
- MUST reference specific rules from domain-xxx skill
- Explain WHY this domain has this constraint

### Recommended Solution
- Provide solution that follows domain best practices
- Not just fixing the compile error

## EXAMPLE: Web API + Send Error

Question: "Web API config sharing error: Rc cannot be sent"

CORRECT Response:
```
### Reasoning Chain
+-- Layer 1: Send/Sync Error
|   Problem: Rc<T> cannot be sent between threads
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: Web Domain (domain-web)
|   Constraint: Handlers run on any thread
|   Rule: Shared state must be thread-safe
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: Use Arc<T> + State extractor

### Domain Constraints Analysis
From domain-web:
- "Rc in state" is a Common Mistake
- Web handlers require Send + Sync
- Recommended: axum State<Arc<T>> pattern

### Recommended Solution
[Code following Web best practices]
```

WRONG Response (stops at L1):
```
Problem: Rc is not Send
Solution: Use Arc
```

## SKILLS TO INVOKE

Always invoke with Skill() tool:
- Skill(rust-router) - First, to get routing
- Skill(m0x-xxx) - Layer 1 skill based on error
- Skill(domain-xxx) - Layer 3 skill based on domain keywords

===================================

{"assignees":[],"body":"**Epic: #221 (Agnostic selection components)**

## Goal
Complex editable tag chips with add/remove/reorder, validation, collection-based keyboard navigation, form integration.

## Out of scope
Framework-specific adapter components (Leptos/Dioxus).

## Spec refs
- `spec/components/selection/tags-input.md`
- `spec/shared/selection-patterns.md`

## Depends on
- Epic #53 (Collections)

## Layer
Component

## Framework
None

## Test tier
Unit

## Fibonacci points
8

## Element/ref handling note

TagsInput agnostic code should own tag keys, input value, highlight/removal state, active-descendant semantics, and ARIA/data attrs. Live tag/input focus, selection/caret reads, scroll-into-view, and option list positioning must be adapter-resolved with native handles.

Stable string IDs remain valid for semantic ARIA relationships, hydration-stable markup, and `data-ars-*` hooks. They should not be used as a substitute for live element handles. Leptos adapters should prefer `NodeRef`/resolved DOM elements for live operations; Dioxus adapters should prefer `MountedData` or the Dioxus platform layer where available.

## Tests to add first
- Add tag on Enter/comma.
- Remove tag via delete button or Backspace.
- Tag navigation with arrow keys.
- Duplicate prevention.
- Max tags limit.
- Validation (on_validate callback).
- ConnectApi produces tag list with `role="listbox"` or grid pattern.
- Each tag with remove button.
- Input field attributes.
- Form integration with multiple hidden inputs.

## Acceptance criteria
- All public types documented per workspace `missing_docs` lint.
- State machine matches spec §1 exactly.
- ConnectApi produces correct ARIA attributes per spec §2.
- Snapshot tests cover the component's `connect()` / `Api` `AttrMap` output for every anatomy part and each output-affecting state, prop, or context branch, with committed `.snap` fixtures passing `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject`.
- All named tests pass with zero warnings.

## Spec impact
No spec change required.

## Board update rule
If blocked, keep `Status` in `Todo`, use native GitHub issue dependencies, and name the dependency or spec ambiguity.

content.text: Dependency #53 is closed. Let me read the spec and explore existing component patterns in parallel.

content.content:
# Spec Directory Guide

## File Conventions

- `_category.md` files contain category overviews and shared conventions
- Component files use kebab-case matching the component name
- Shared types in `shared/` are referenced by component frontmatter
- Headings are numbered (`## 1. State Machine`, `### 1.1 States`)

## Spec Changes During Implementation

The repo is in active implementation. Spec edits inside `spec/` should usually be tied to a concrete GitHub implementation task or PR, not made as detached cleanup.

- Read the implementation issue before changing spec text.
- Keep cited spec sections synchronized with the implementation task that discovered the mismatch.
- If an adapter-specific implementation exposes a missing shared abstraction, promote that abstraction into the appropriate foundation/shared spec instead of documenting it only in framework code.
- Do not leave placeholder notes for later reconciliation; resolve the spec/code mismatch in the same task whenever possible.

## Adding a New Component

1. Create `components/{category}/{component-name}.md` with YAML frontmatter
2. Follow the section structure defined in `foundation/10-component-spec-template.md` for the appropriate tier
3. Add an entry to `manifest.toml` with path, category, deps, and related
4. Update `foundation/02-component-catalog.md` with the new component
5. Run `cargo run -p spec-tool -- validate` to verify consistency

## Component File Format

```markdown
---
component: ComponentName
category: { category }
tier: stateless | stateful | complex
foundation_deps: [architecture, accessibility, ...]
shared_deps: [date-time-types, ...]
related: [sibling-component, ...]
---

# ComponentName
```

See `foundation/10-component-spec-template.md` for the canonical section structure, ordering rules, conformance checklist, and skeleton examples for each tier.

I'm implementing a new framework-agnostic component `TagsInput` in the `ars-components` crate at `crates/ars-components/src/selection/tags_input/`. I need to understand the existing module layout and conventions by examining an existing complex/complete component in the same crate.

Please explore `crates/ars-components/src/selection/` and report:

1. The full directory/file structure of `crates/ars-components/src/selection/` — list every component subdirectory and whether each uses `mod.rs` (directory-backed modules).
2. Pick the most complete/complex selection component (e.g. combobox, select, listbox, or tags-input if it already exists) and show me its `mod.rs` structure: how the State/Event/Context/Props/Machine/Api/Part/Messages are organized into files (is it one mod.rs or split into submodules like state.rs, machine.rs, api.rs, messages.rs?).
3. How is the component registered in `crates/ars-components/src/selection/mod.rs` and `crates/ars-components/src/lib.rs`?
4. Show the exact import statements used at the top of an existing component's machine/api file (so I know the canonical paths for `Machine`, `TransitionPlan`, `PendingEffect`, `Bindable`, `ComponentIds`, `AttrMap`, `HtmlAttr`, `AriaAttr`, `ConnectApi`, `ComponentPart` derive, `HasId` derive, `MessageFn`, `ComponentMessages`, `Env`, `Locale`, `use_platform_effects`, `no_cleanup`, `KeyboardEventData`, `KeyboardKey`).
5. Check if `crates/ars-components/src/selection/tags_input/` or any tags-input file already exists.

Report file paths and concrete code excerpts. Do not modify anything.

I'm implementing a framework-agnostic state-machine component in the `ars-components` crate. I need to understand the exact signatures and APIs of the core primitives it depends on. Explore `crates/ars-core`, `crates/ars-interactions`, `crates/ars-collections`, `crates/ars-i18n`, and report concrete signatures (with file paths) for:

1. `ars_core::Machine` trait — full trait definition including associated types (State, Event, Context, Props, Api, Messages) and methods (`init`, `transition`, `connect`, `initial_effects`). What are the exact parameter types of `init` (does it take `&Env` and `&Messages`)?
2. `TransitionPlan<M>` — its constructors and builder methods: `context_only`, `to`, `apply`, `with_effect`. Exact signatures.
3. `PendingEffect<M>` — `PendingEffect::new(name, closure)` signature. What are the closure's parameters `(ctx, props, send)` types and return type? What is `no_cleanup()` and where is it?
4. `Bindable<T>` — `controlled`, `uncontrolled`, `get`, `set` method signatures. Which module?
5. `ComponentIds` — `from_id`, `part`, `item` method signatures. Note: spec calls `ctx.ids.item("tag", &idx)` (taking &usize) AND `ctx.ids.item("tag", index)` (taking usize) in different places — what is the actual signature of `item`? Which module?
6. `AttrMap` — `new`, `set`, `set_bool` signatures. `HtmlAttr` enum variants used: `Data(&str)`, `Id`, `For`, `Role`, `Type`, `Name`, `Value`, `Placeholder`, `Disabled`, `ReadOnly`, `Required`, `MaxLength`, `TabIndex`, `Aria(AriaAttr)`. `AriaAttr` variants: `LabelledBy`, `Label`, `Description`, `DescribedBy`, `Disabled`, `Required`, `Hidden`, `Live`. Confirm these all exist with exact names. Which crate/module?
7. `ConnectApi` trait — definition (`type Part`, `fn part_attrs`).
8. The `ComponentPart` derive macro and `#[scope = "..."]` attribute — how does it generate `data_attrs()` returning `[(attr, val); 2]`? Show an example Part enum with struct variants like `Tag { index: usize }` and how `data_attrs()` handles them.
9. `HasId` derive macro — what does it require (a `pub id: String` field)?
10. `MessageFn` — `new`, `static_str` constructors. `ComponentMessages` trait. Which crate?
11. `KeyboardEventData` and `KeyboardKey` — where defined, what variants (Enter, Backspace, Delete, ArrowLeft, ArrowRight, Escape)? Field `key` on KeyboardEventData?
12. `use_platform_effects()` and the `focus_element_by_id` method — signatures and module.
13. `Env` and `Locale` types — where defined.

Report exact signatures and file paths. Do not modify anything.

I'm implementing a new framework-agnostic component `TagsInput` in `crates/ars-components` and need to understand the test conventions. Explore the test setup and report concrete examples with file paths:

1. Unit tests: where do per-component unit tests live for `ars-components`? Inline `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` in the component file, or under `crates/ars-components/tests/`? Show an example test for an existing complex component (combobox/select/listbox/gridlist).

2. Snapshot tests (insta): How are `connect()`/`Api` `AttrMap` snapshot tests written? Show an example test that snapshots a component's part attributes. Where do `.snap` files live? How is `AttrMap` serialized for snapshotting (is there a helper, a Debug/Display impl, or a serde serialization)? What does `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` expect?

3. Spec-conformance tests: There's a "spec-conformance" gate. Where do these tests live and what do they check (Part enum anatomy, public API surface)? Show an example.

4. Proptest: `crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/` — show the `common.rs` helper and an example proptest for an existing state machine.

5. Coverage thresholds: find `ars-components` threshold in `xtask` (default_thresholds) — what line/branch coverage is required?

6. How do existing tests construct a Machine and drive transitions? Show the helper/pattern to call `Machine::init`, `Machine::transition`, apply a `TransitionPlan` to context, and call `Machine::connect` to get the `Api`. Is there a `Service` test harness? Show exact usage.

Report exact code excerpts and file paths. Do not modify anything.

### 1. Directory Structure of `crates/ars-components/src/selection/`

│   └── snapshots/

│   └── snapshots/
├── listbox/

│   └── snapshots/

content.text: I now have a complete picture. Before finalizing the plan, I've identified several spec drift/inconsistency items I must resolve in this PR. Two are architecturally forced (non-negotiable): the `Machine` trait requires a `type Effect` (the spec omits it and uses string names), and agnostic `ars-components` cannot call the adapter-only `use_platform_effects()` — the canonical pattern (per `pin_input`) is a typed `Effect` enum resolved by the adapter via `ctx`. Two others are genuine scope choices. Let me confirm those with you.

questions.question: The spec has internal drift on inline tag editing: §1.5 says Context should carry an `editing_draft: String` field updated on each keystroke, but §1.3's Context struct and the §1.6 machine impl never declare or wire it. How should I handle inline editing in the agnostic core?

options.label: Add & fully wire editing_draft
options.description: Add `editing_draft` to Context; EditTag initializes it from the tag value, InputChange-while-editing updates it, CommitEdit/CancelEdit clear it. Agnostic core owns the inline-edit value (matches the issue's 'agnostic code should own input value' note). Amend spec §1.3 + §1.6 to match.

options.label: Omit editing_draft
options.description: Implement Context/machine exactly as §1.3/§1.6 (no editing_draft); the adapter holds the inline draft in the DOM and passes it via CommitEdit{value}. Amend spec §1.5 to remove the editing_draft reference.

questions.question: Spec §3.3 describes screen-reader live-region announcements ('Removed {tag}', 'Maximum of {max} tags reached'), but §4.1 Messages has no message fields for them and the §1.6 machine emits no announcement effect. How should the agnostic core handle these?

options.label: Adapter concern (no new core API)
options.description: Treat §3.3 announcements as adapter-level a11y wiring. The ErrorMessage part already exposes aria-live=polite. Add no new Messages fields or effects to the agnostic core now. Note the reasoning in the PR; revisit if the post-implementation audit flags it.

options.label: Add announcement Messages + effect
options.description: Extend Messages with `removed_announcement`/`max_reached_announcement` fields and emit an `Announce` effect from RemoveTag/RemoveTagAtIndex/AddTag so the core fully owns announcement text. Larger surface; amend spec §4.1 + §1.6 accordingly.

content.text:
All design questions are resolved. I have the canonical patterns: typed `Effect` enum resolved by the adapter via `ctx` (like `pin_input`'s `Effect::FocusCell`), a `LiveRegion` part for announcements (like `combobox`), and the typed-callback effect convention. I also found one more issue-driven gap: the issue requires an "add on comma" test, but the spec's keydown handler only adds on Enter (delimiter-on-type isn't wired; only Paste splits) — the IME note implies it should be. I'll reconcile that too.

Let me write the plan file.

input.content:
# Task #242 — Implement TagsInput agnostic core

## Context

GitHub issue #242 (Epic #221, Agnostic selection components, 8 pts, Unit test tier) asks for the
framework-agnostic core of `TagsInput`: a text input that converts entries into removable
tag chips with add/remove/reorder, duplicate prevention, max-tags, inline editing, paste,
collection-style keyboard navigation, IME handling, and form integration. Adapter components
(Leptos/Dioxus) are explicitly **out of scope** for this task.

Spec: `spec/components/selection/tags-input.md` (tier: complex, category: selection).
Dependency Epic #53 (Collections) is **closed** — unblocked.

The component does **not** yet exist (`crates/ars-components/src/selection/tags_input/` absent).
The deliverable is one `mod.rs` following the established single-file component convention used
by every other selection component (combobox, listbox, select, etc.).

### Spec drift to resolve in this PR (per the workspace spec-sync rule)

The spec's §1.6 code cannot be implemented verbatim; reconciling it is part of the task. The
amendments below land in `spec/components/selection/tags-input.md` **in this same PR**:

1. **`type Effect` missing + adapter-only calls in agnostic closures.** §1.6 omits the
   `Machine::Effect` associated type (the trait requires `type Effect: EffectName`) and its
   effect closures call `use_platform_effects()` / `focus_element_by_id()` — both live in the
   *adapter* crates and are unavailable in agnostic `ars-components`. Canonical pattern
   (`crates/ars-components/src/input/pin_input/mod.rs`, `Effect::FocusCell`): define a typed
   `Effect` enum and emit `PendingEffect::named(Effect::X)`; the adapter resolves DOM focus from
   `ctx` (`focused_tag` / `editing_tag`). Replace all `use_platform_effects()` effect closures.

2. **Duplicate `Api` methods.** §1.7 defines `on_input_focus`, `on_input_blur`,
   `on_input_change`, `on_tag_delete`, `on_clear_click` twice (≈ lines 639-652 and 871-939) —
   will not compile. Keep one set (the richer "Event handlers" block) and amend the spec.

3. **`editing_draft` (confirmed: add & fully wire).** §1.5 says Context carries
   `editing_draft: String` but §1.3/§1.6 never declare/wire it. Add the field; `EditTag` inits it
   from the tag value, `InputChange`-while-`EditingTag` updates it, `CommitEdit`/`CancelEdit`
   clear it. Agnostic core owns the inline-edit value. Amend §1.3 + §1.6.

4. **Live-region announcements (confirmed: add Messages + effect).** §3.3 describes
   "Removed {tag}" / "Maximum of {max} tags reached" announcements with no backing API. Add a
   `LiveRegion` part (aria-live=polite), two `Messages` fields, a `live_message: String` Context
   field set in the relevant transitions, and an `Effect::Announce` (adapter surfaces
   `ctx.live_message`). Amend §1.3, §1.6, §1.7, §2 anatomy, §3.3, §4.1.

5. **Delimiter-on-type (issue requires an "add on comma" test).** §1.6's `InputChange` only sets
   `input_value`; only `Paste` splits on the delimiter. The §1.2 IME note ("delimiter detection
   MUST be suppressed during composition") implies delimiter detection happens during typing. Wire
   `InputChange` (when not composing and not editing) to split completed `delimiter`-separated
   segments into tags and keep the trailing remainder as `input_value`. Amend §1.6.

6. **Validation.** The issue lists an "on_validate callback" test, but the spec routes validation
   to the forms layer (§5 `PerTagValidator`) — core `Props` has no validate callback (the
   `// Change callbacks provided by the adapter layer` comment). Honor the spec: test the `invalid`
   prop / `aria-invalid` / ErrorMessage wiring instead of inventing a core callback.

## Files to create / modify

- **Create** `crates/ars-components/src/selection/tags_input/mod.rs` — the whole component:
  `State`, `Event`, `Context`, `Props` (+`#[derive(HasId)]` and builder methods matching the
  combobox/text_field convention), `BlurBehavior`, `Messages` (+`ComponentMessages`), `Effect`
  enum, `Part` (`#[derive(ComponentPart)] #[scope = "tags-input"]`), `Machine` (`impl
  ars_core::Machine`), `Api<'a>` + `impl ConnectApi`, effect helper fns, and inline
  `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (unit + snapshot).
- **Modify** `crates/ars-components/src/selection/mod.rs` — add `pub mod tags_input;` (alphabetical).
- **Modify** `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/selection.rs` — add
  `tags_input` to imports and a `tags_input_anatomy_matches_spec` test via `assert_anatomy`.
- **Modify** `crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/selection.rs` — add `tags_input`
  generators + an `#[ignore]` proptest of event-sequence invariants.
- **Modify** `spec/components/selection/tags-input.md` — land amendments 1-6 above.
- **Snapshots** auto-created under `crates/ars-components/src/selection/tags_input/snapshots/`.

## Canonical APIs to reuse (verified)

- Imports from `ars_core`: `AriaAttr, AttrMap, Bindable, ComponentIds, ComponentMessages,
  ComponentPart, ConnectApi, Env, HtmlAttr, KeyboardKey, Locale, MessageFn, PendingEffect,
  TransitionPlan, no_cleanup` (+ `ConnectApi as _`, `Service` in tests). `KeyboardEventData` from
  `ars_interactions`. Core trait/impl signatures confirmed in `crates/ars-core/src/lib.rs`.
- `Bindable<Vec<String>>` is valid (blanket `impl<T: Clone+PartialEq+Debug> BindableValue for T`).
- `TransitionPlan::context_only(f)` / `::to(state).apply(f)` / `.with_effect(PendingEffect::named(..))`.
- `ComponentIds::part(&str)` and `item(&str, &impl Display)` (accepts `usize`/`&usize`).
- `AttrMap::new()/set/set_bool`; all `HtmlAttr`/`AriaAttr` variants used in §1.7 exist.
- `MessageFn::new(closure)` and `MessageFn::static_str(&'static str)`.
- Effect convention: typed enum + `PendingEffect::named(Effect::X)`, adapter reads `ctx`
  (model: `crates/ars-components/src/input/pin_input/mod.rs`).
- Callback-style effects with props closures: `crates/ars-components/src/input/text_field/mod.rs`
  (`focus_change_effect`). LiveRegion part + on-demand text Api method:
  `crates/ars-components/src/selection/combobox/mod.rs` (`live_region_attrs`, `results_count_text`).

## Design summary

- **States** (§1.1): `Idle`, `Focused`, `EditingTag { index }`.
- **Context** (§1.3 + amendments): spec fields **plus** `editing_draft: String` and
  `live_message: String`. `value: Bindable<Vec<String>>` (Vec preserves insertion order; dup
  prevention in transition logic — tags are positional strings, not keyed `Collection` items, so
  parts key on `index: usize` exactly as the spec models).
- **Effect** enum: `FocusTag` (focus `ctx.focused_tag`), `FocusInput`, `FocusEditInput`
  (focus `ctx.editing_tag`), `Announce` (surface `ctx.live_message`).
- **Part** enum (13): `Root, Label, Control, Tag{index}, TagText{index}, TagDeleteTrigger{index},
  TagEdit{index}, Input, ClearTrigger, HiddenInput, Description, ErrorMessage, LiveRegion`.
- **Transitions**: implement §1.6 exactly except the reconciliations — disabled/readonly guards;
  `AddTag` (trim, max, dup); `RemoveTag`/`RemoveTagAtIndex` (set `live_message` via
  `removed_announcement`, refocus adjacent tag via `FocusTag`, or `FocusInput`+`Announce` when
  empty); `EditTag`/`CommitEdit`/`CancelEdit` (editable/disabled/readonly guard, `editing_draft`);
  `Focus`/`Blur` (+ `EditingTag` blur commit + `BlurBehavior`); `InputChange` (delimiter-on-type
  split when not composing/editing; else `editing_draft`); `Paste`; `ClearAll`; `FocusPrevTag`/
  `FocusNextTag`; `CompositionStart`/`End`; `AddTag` blocked by max → `Announce` max-reached.
- **Api / connect** (§1.7): one method per part returning `AttrMap` exactly per §1.6 (grid pattern:
  `Control` role=grid, `Tag` role=row, `TagText`/`TagDeleteTrigger` role=gridcell), plus
  `count_text()`, `live_region_attrs()`, an announcement-text accessor, deduped event handlers, and
  `impl ConnectApi { fn part_attrs }`.

## Test plan (TDD — write tests first, per issue "Tests to add first")

Inline unit tests in `mod.rs` (drive via `Service::<Machine>::new(props, &Env::default(),
&Messages::default())`, `service.send(event)`, `service.connect(&send)`):
add-on-Enter; add-on-comma (delimiter-on-type); remove via delete button (`RemoveTag`) and via
Backspace (empty input → focus last; focused tag → remove); arrow-key tag navigation; duplicate
prevention; max-tags limit (+ max-reached announcement); `invalid` state attrs + ErrorMessage;
inline edit (`EditTag`/`CommitEdit`/`CancelEdit` + `editing_draft`); paste split; clear-all; IME
suppression (Enter/delimiter no-op while composing); blur behavior (`Add`/`Clear`); removal
announcement live region.

Snapshot tests (`insta::assert_snapshot!` over a `snapshot_attrs(&attrs)` Debug helper, matching
the checkbox/combobox pattern): every anatomy part **and** each output-affecting state/prop/context
branch — root (default/disabled/readonly/invalid/focused), control (±max), tag
(focused/editing/disabled/focus-visible), tag_text, tag_delete_trigger (±disabled), tag_edit
(editing/hidden), input (placeholder/disabled/readonly/required/max_length/at-max/invalid-describedby),
clear_trigger (±disabled), hidden_input (name/required/disabled), description, error_message
(visible/hidden), live_region. Commit `.snap` fixtures; must pass
`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject`. Attach `snapshot-reviewed` label to the PR.

Spec-conformance: `tags_input_anatomy_matches_spec` enumerating all 13 parts in declared order.

Proptest: `arb_*` props/events + an `#[ignore]` sequence test asserting invariants — no duplicates
when `!allow_duplicates`, `len <= max`, `focused_tag`/`editing_tag` in range, `hidden_input` value
== `value.join(delimiter)`, stable id.

## Verification

1. `cargo test -p ars-components tags_input` (unit + snapshot) and
   `cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject` — review/accept new `.snap` files.
2. `cargo test -p ars-components --test spec_conformance tags_input_anatomy_matches_spec`.
3. `cargo test -p ars-components --test proptest_state_machines` (sanity; `#[ignore]` proptest is
   nightly-driven).
4. Run the mandatory `post-implementation-audit` skill (all three phases — spec/impl drift,
   iterative "anything missing?", test coverage); fix every finding in this PR.
5. After user approval: `cargo xci-fast` (fmt, clippy, unit/integration/adapter, snapshot-count,
   native coverage ≥ 90/90 for `ars-components`) — fix failures before push.
6. Open PR targeting `main` with `Closes #242`; add `snapshot-reviewed` label; then run the
   `waiting-for-codex-review` skill loop until Codex 👍.

## Out of scope

Leptos/Dioxus adapter components, adapter tests, widgets examples, E2E fixtures (those are a
separate adapter task per `docs/implementation/adapter-component-delivery.md`). Mutation testing is
nightly-driven, not a delivery gate.

- **Snapshots** auto-created under `crates/ars-components/src/selection/tags_input/snapshots/`.

## Canonical APIs to reuse (verified)

- Imports from `ars_core`: `AriaAttr, AttrMap, Bindable, ComponentIds, ComponentMessages,
  ComponentPart, ConnectApi, Env, HtmlAttr, KeyboardKey, Locale, MessageFn, PendingEffect,
  TransitionPlan, no_cleanup` (+ `ConnectApi as _`, `Service` in tests). `KeyboardEventData` from
  `ars_interactions`. Core trait/impl signatures confirmed in `crates/ars-core/src/lib.rs`.
- `Bindable<Vec<String>>` is valid (blanket `impl<T: Clone+PartialEq+Debug> BindableValue for T`).
- `TransitionPlan::context_only(f)` / `::to(state).apply(f)` / `.with_effect(PendingEffect::named(..))`.
- `ComponentIds::part(&str)` and `item(&str, &impl Display)` (accepts `usize`/`&usize`).
- `AttrMap::new()/set/set_bool`; all `HtmlAttr`/`AriaAttr` variants used in §1.7 exist.
- `MessageFn::new(closure)` and `MessageFn::static_str(&'static str)`.
- Effect convention: typed enum + `PendingEffect::named(Effect::X)`, adapter reads `ctx`
  (model: `crates/ars-components/src/input/pin_input/mod.rs`).
- Callback-style effects with props closures: `crates/ars-components/src/input/text_field/mod.rs`
  (`focus_change_effect`). LiveRegion part + on-demand text Api method:
  `crates/ars-components/src/selection/combobox/mod.rs` (`live_region_attrs`, `results_count_text`).

## Design summary

- **States** (§1.1): `Idle`, `Focused`, `EditingTag { index }`.
- **Context** (§1.3 + amendments): spec fields **plus** `editing_draft: String` and
  `live_message: String`. `value: Bindable<Vec<String>>` (Vec preserves insertion order; dup
  prevention in transition logic — tags are positional strings, not keyed `Collection` items, so
  parts key on `index: usize` exactly as the spec models).
- **Effect** enum: `FocusTag` (focus `ctx.focused_tag`), `FocusInput`, `FocusEditInput`
  (focus `ctx.editing_tag`), `Announce` (surface `ctx.live_message`).
- **Part** enum (13): `Root, Label, Control, Tag{index}, TagText{index}, TagDeleteTrigger{index},
  TagEdit{index}, Input, ClearTrigger, HiddenInput, Description, ErrorMessage, LiveRegion`.
- **Transitions**: implement §1.6 exactly except the reconciliations — disabled/readonly guards;
  `AddTag` (trim, max, dup); `RemoveTag`/`RemoveTagAtIndex` (set `live_message` via
  `removed_announcement`, refocus adjacent tag via `FocusTag`, or `FocusInput`+`Announce` when
  empty); `EditTag`/`CommitEdit`/`CancelEdit` (editable/disabled/readonly guard, `editing_draft`);
  `Focus`/`Blur` (+ `EditingTag` blur commit + `BlurBehavior`); `InputChange` (delimiter-on-type
  split when not composing/editing; else `editing_draft`); `Paste`; `ClearAll`; `FocusPrevTag`/
  `FocusNextTag`; `CompositionStart`/`End`; `AddTag` blocked by max → `Announce` max-reached.
- **Api / connect** (§1.7): one method per part returning `AttrMap` exactly per §1.6 (grid pattern:
  `Control` role=grid, `Tag` role=row, `TagText`/`TagDeleteTrigger` role=gridcell), plus
  `count_text()`, `live_region_attrs()`, an announcement-text accessor, deduped event handlers, and
  `impl ConnectApi { fn part_attrs }`.

## Test plan (TDD — write tests first, per issue "Tests to add first")

Inline unit tests in `mod.rs` (drive via `Service::<Machine>::new(props, &Env::default(),
&Messages::default())`, `service.send(event)`, `service.connect(&send)`):
add-on-Enter; add-on-comma (delimiter-on-type); remove via delete button (`RemoveTag`) and via
Backspace (empty input → focus last; focused tag → remove); arrow-key tag navigation; duplicate
prevention; max-tags limit (+ max-reached announcement); `invalid` state attrs + ErrorMessage;
inline edit (`EditTag`/`CommitEdit`/`CancelEdit` + `editing_draft`); paste split; clear-all; IME
suppression (Enter/delimiter no-op while composing); blur behavior (`Add`/`Clear`); removal
announcement live region.

Snapshot tests (`insta::assert_snapshot!` over a `snapshot_attrs(&attrs)` Debug helper, matching
the checkbox/combobox pattern): every anatomy part **and** each output-affecting state/prop/context
branch — root (default/disabled/readonly/invalid/focused), control (±max), tag
(focused/editing/disabled/focus-visible), tag_text, tag_delete_trigger (±disabled), tag_edit
(editing/hidden), input (placeholder/disabled/readonly/required/max_length/at-max/invalid-describedby),
clear_trigger (±disabled), hidden_input (name/required/disabled), description, error_message
(visible/hidden), live_region. Commit `.snap` fixtures; must pass
`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject`. Attach `snapshot-reviewed` label to the PR.

Spec-conformance: `tags_input_anatomy_matches_spec` enumerating all 13 parts in declared order.

Proptest: `arb_*` props/events + an `#[ignore]` sequence test asserting invariants — no duplicates
when `!allow_duplicates`, `len <= max`, `focused_tag`/`editing_tag` in range, `hidden_input` value
== `value.join(delimiter)`, stable id.

## Verification

1. `cargo test -p ars-components tags_input` (unit + snapshot) and
   `cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject` — review/accept new `.snap` files.
2. `cargo test -p ars-components --test spec_conformance tags_input_anatomy_matches_spec`.
3. `cargo test -p ars-components --test proptest_state_machines` (sanity; `#[ignore]` proptest is
   nightly-driven).
4. Run the mandatory `post-implementation-audit` skill (all three phases — spec/impl drift,
   iterative "anything missing?", test coverage); fix every finding in this PR.
5. After user approval: `cargo xci-fast` (fmt, clippy, unit/integration/adapter, snapshot-count,
   native coverage ≥ 90/90 for `ars-components`) — fix failures before push.
6. Open PR targeting `main` with `Closes #242`; add `snapshot-reviewed` label; then run the
   `waiting-for-codex-review` skill loop until Codex 👍.

## Out of scope

Leptos/Dioxus adapter components, adapter tests, widgets examples, E2E fixtures (those are a
separate adapter task per `docs/implementation/adapter-component-delivery.md`). Mutation testing is
nightly-driven, not a delivery gate.

# Task #242 — Implement TagsInput agnostic core

## Context

GitHub issue #242 (Epic #221, Agnostic selection components, 8 pts, Unit test tier) asks for the
framework-agnostic core of `TagsInput`: a text input that converts entries into removable
tag chips with add/remove/reorder, duplicate prevention, max-tags, inline editing, paste,
collection-style keyboard navigation, IME handling, and form integration. Adapter components
(Leptos/Dioxus) are explicitly **out of scope** for this task.

Spec: `spec/components/selection/tags-input.md` (tier: complex, category: selection).
Dependency Epic #53 (Collections) is **closed** — unblocked.

The component does **not** yet exist (`crates/ars-components/src/selection/tags_input/` absent).
The deliverable is one `mod.rs` following the established single-file component convention used
by every other selection component (combobox, listbox, select, etc.).

### Spec drift to resolve in this PR (per the workspace spec-sync rule)

The spec's §1.6 code cannot be implemented verbatim; reconciling it is part of the task. The
amendments below land in `spec/components/selection/tags-input.md` **in this same PR**:

1. **`type Effect` missing + adapter-only calls in agnostic closures.** §1.6 omits the
   `Machine::Effect` associated type (the trait requires `type Effect: EffectName`) and its
   effect closures call `use_platform_effects()` / `focus_element_by_id()` — both live in the
   *adapter* crates and are unavailable in agnostic `ars-components`. Canonical pattern
   (`crates/ars-components/src/input/pin_input/mod.rs`, `Effect::FocusCell`): define a typed
   `Effect` enum and emit `PendingEffect::named(Effect::X)`; the adapter resolves DOM focus from
   `ctx` (`focused_tag` / `editing_tag`). Replace all `use_platform_effects()` effect closures.

2. **Duplicate `Api` methods.** §1.7 defines `on_input_focus`, `on_input_blur`,
   `on_input_change`, `on_tag_delete`, `on_clear_click` twice (≈ lines 639-652 and 871-939) —
   will not compile. Keep one set (the richer "Event handlers" block) and amend the spec.

3. **`editing_draft` (confirmed: add & fully wire).** §1.5 says Context carries
   `editing_draft: String` but §1.3/§1.6 never declare/wire it. Add the field; `EditTag` inits it
   from the tag value, `InputChange`-while-`EditingTag` updates it, `CommitEdit`/`CancelEdit`
   clear it. Agnostic core owns the inline-edit value. Amend §1.3 + §1.6.

4. **Live-region announcements (confirmed: add Messages + effect).** §3.3 describes
   "Removed {tag}" / "Maximum of {max} tags reached" announcements with no backing API. Add a
   `LiveRegion` part (aria-live=polite), two `Messages` fields, a `live_message: String` Context
   field set in the relevant transitions, and an `Effect::Announce` (adapter surfaces
   `ctx.live_message`). Amend §1.3, §1.6, §1.7, §2 anatomy, §3.3, §4.1.

5. **Delimiter-on-type (issue requires an "add on comma" test).** §1.6's `InputChange` only sets
   `input_value`; only `Paste` splits on the delimiter. The §1.2 IME note ("delimiter detection
   MUST be suppressed during composition") implies delimiter detection happens during typing. Wire
   `InputChange` (when not composing and not editing) to split completed `delimiter`-separated
   segments into tags and keep the trailing remainder as `input_value`. Amend §1.6.

6. **Validation.** The issue lists an "on_validate callback" test, but the spec routes validation
   to the forms layer (§5 `PerTagValidator`) — core `Props` has no validate callback (the
   `// Change callbacks provided by the adapter layer` comment). Honor the spec: test the `invalid`
   prop / `aria-invalid` / ErrorMessage wiring instead of inventing a core callback.

## Files to create / modify

- **Create** `crates/ars-components/src/selection/tags_input/mod.rs` — the whole component:
  `State`, `Event`, `Context`, `Props` (+`#[derive(HasId)]` and builder methods matching the
  combobox/text_field convention), `BlurBehavior`, `Messages` (+`ComponentMessages`), `Effect`
  enum, `Part` (`#[derive(ComponentPart)] #[scope = "tags-input"]`), `Machine` (`impl
  ars_core::Machine`), `Api<'a>` + `impl ConnectApi`, effect helper fns, and inline
  `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (unit + snapshot).
- **Modify** `crates/ars-components/src/selection/mod.rs` — add `pub mod tags_input;` (alphabetical).
- **Modify** `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/selection.rs` — add
  `tags_input` to imports and a `tags_input_anatomy_matches_spec` test via `assert_anatomy`.
- **Modify** `crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/selection.rs` — add `tags_input`
  generators + an `#[ignore]` proptest of event-sequence invariants.
- **Modify** `spec/components/selection/tags-input.md` — land amendments 1-6 above.
- **Snapshots** auto-created under `crates/ars-components/src/selection/tags_input/snapshots/`.

## Canonical APIs to reuse (verified)

- Imports from `ars_core`: `AriaAttr, AttrMap, Bindable, ComponentIds, ComponentMessages,
  ComponentPart, ConnectApi, Env, HtmlAttr, KeyboardKey, Locale, MessageFn, PendingEffect,
  TransitionPlan, no_cleanup` (+ `ConnectApi as _`, `Service` in tests). `KeyboardEventData` from
  `ars_interactions`. Core trait/impl signatures confirmed in `crates/ars-core/src/lib.rs`.
- `Bindable<Vec<String>>` is valid (blanket `impl<T: Clone+PartialEq+Debug> BindableValue for T`).
- `TransitionPlan::context_only(f)` / `::to(state).apply(f)` / `.with_effect(PendingEffect::named(..))`.
- `ComponentIds::part(&str)` and `item(&str, &impl Display)` (accepts `usize`/`&usize`).
- `AttrMap::new()/set/set_bool`; all `HtmlAttr`/`AriaAttr` variants used in §1.7 exist.
- `MessageFn::new(closure)` and `MessageFn::static_str(&'static str)`.
- Effect convention: typed enum + `PendingEffect::named(Effect::X)`, adapter reads `ctx`
  (model: `crates/ars-components/src/input/pin_input/mod.rs`).
- Callback-style effects with props closures: `crates/ars-components/src/input/text_field/mod.rs`
  (`focus_change_effect`). LiveRegion part + on-demand text Api method:
  `crates/ars-components/src/selection/combobox/mod.rs` (`live_region_attrs`, `results_count_text`).

  `Control` role=grid, `Tag` role=row, `TagText`/`TagDeleteTrigger` role=gridcell), plus
  `count_text()`, `live_region_attrs()`, an announcement-text accessor, deduped event handlers, and
  `impl ConnectApi { fn part_attrs }`.

## Test plan (TDD — write tests first, per issue "Tests to add first")

Inline unit tests in `mod.rs` (drive via `Service::<Machine>::new(props, &Env::default(),
&Messages::default())`, `service.send(event)`, `service.connect(&send)`):
add-on-Enter; add-on-comma (delimiter-on-type); remove via delete button (`RemoveTag`) and via
Backspace (empty input → focus last; focused tag → remove); arrow-key tag navigation; duplicate
prevention; max-tags limit (+ max-reached announcement); `invalid` state attrs + ErrorMessage;
inline edit (`EditTag`/`CommitEdit`/`CancelEdit` + `editing_draft`); paste split; clear-all; IME
suppression (Enter/delimiter no-op while composing); blur behavior (`Add`/`Clear`); removal
announcement live region.

Snapshot tests (`insta::assert_snapshot!` over a `snapshot_attrs(&attrs)` Debug helper, matching
the checkbox/combobox pattern): every anatomy part **and** each output-affecting state/prop/context
branch — root (default/disabled/readonly/invalid/focused), control (±max), tag
(focused/editing/disabled/focus-visible), tag_text, tag_delete_trigger (±disabled), tag_edit
(editing/hidden), input (placeholder/disabled/readonly/required/max_length/at-max/invalid-describedby),
clear_trigger (±disabled), hidden_input (name/required/disabled), description, error_message
(visible/hidden), live_region. Commit `.snap` fixtures; must pass
`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject`. Attach `snapshot-reviewed` label to the PR.

Spec-conformance: `tags_input_anatomy_matches_spec` enumerating all 13 parts in declared order.

Proptest: `arb_*` props/events + an `#[ignore]` sequence test asserting invariants — no duplicates
when `!allow_duplicates`, `len <= max`, `focused_tag`/`editing_tag` in range, `hidden_input` value
== `value.join(delimiter)`, stable id.

## Verification

1. `cargo test -p ars-components tags_input` (unit + snapshot) and
   `cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject` — review/accept new `.snap` files.
2. `cargo test -p ars-components --test spec_conformance tags_input_anatomy_matches_spec`.
3. `cargo test -p ars-components --test proptest_state_machines` (sanity; `#[ignore]` proptest is
   nightly-driven).
4. Run the mandatory `post-implementation-audit` skill (all three phases — spec/impl drift,
   iterative "anything missing?", test coverage); fix every finding in this PR.
5. After user approval: `cargo xci-fast` (fmt, clippy, unit/integration/adapter, snapshot-count,
   native coverage ≥ 90/90 for `ars-components`) — fix failures before push.
6. Open PR targeting `main` with `Closes #242`; add `snapshot-reviewed` label; then run the
   `waiting-for-codex-review` skill loop until Codex 👍.

## Out of scope

Leptos/Dioxus adapter components, adapter tests, widgets examples, E2E fixtures (those are a
separate adapter task per `docs/implementation/adapter-component-delivery.md`). Mutation testing is
nightly-driven, not a delivery gate.

toolUseResult.plan:
# Task #242 — Implement TagsInput agnostic core

## Context

GitHub issue #242 (Epic #221, Agnostic selection components, 8 pts, Unit test tier) asks for the
framework-agnostic core of `TagsInput`: a text input that converts entries into removable
tag chips with add/remove/reorder, duplicate prevention, max-tags, inline editing, paste,
collection-style keyboard navigation, IME handling, and form integration. Adapter components
(Leptos/Dioxus) are explicitly **out of scope** for this task.

Spec: `spec/components/selection/tags-input.md` (tier: complex, category: selection).
Dependency Epic #53 (Collections) is **closed** — unblocked.

The component does **not** yet exist (`crates/ars-components/src/selection/tags_input/` absent).
The deliverable is one `mod.rs` following the established single-file component convention used
by every other selection component (combobox, listbox, select, etc.).

### Spec drift to resolve in this PR (per the workspace spec-sync rule)

The spec's §1.6 code cannot be implemented verbatim; reconciling it is part of the task. The
amendments below land in `spec/components/selection/tags-input.md` **in this same PR**:

1. **`type Effect` missing + adapter-only calls in agnostic closures.** §1.6 omits the
   `Machine::Effect` associated type (the trait requires `type Effect: EffectName`) and its
   effect closures call `use_platform_effects()` / `focus_element_by_id()` — both live in the
   *adapter* crates and are unavailable in agnostic `ars-components`. Canonical pattern
   (`crates/ars-components/src/input/pin_input/mod.rs`, `Effect::FocusCell`): define a typed
   `Effect` enum and emit `PendingEffect::named(Effect::X)`; the adapter resolves DOM focus from
   `ctx` (`focused_tag` / `editing_tag`). Replace all `use_platform_effects()` effect closures.

2. **Duplicate `Api` methods.** §1.7 defines `on_input_focus`, `on_input_blur`,
   `on_input_change`, `on_tag_delete`, `on_clear_click` twice (≈ lines 639-652 and 871-939) —
   will not compile. Keep one set (the richer "Event handlers" block) and amend the spec.

3. **`editing_draft` (confirmed: add & fully wire).** §1.5 says Context carries
   `editing_draft: String` but §1.3/§1.6 never declare/wire it. Add the field; `EditTag` inits it
   from the tag value, `InputChange`-while-`EditingTag` updates it, `CommitEdit`/`CancelEdit`
   clear it. Agnostic core owns the inline-edit value. Amend §1.3 + §1.6.

4. **Live-region announcements (confirmed: add Messages + effect).** §3.3 describes
   "Removed {tag}" / "Maximum of {max} tags reached" announcements with no backing API. Add a
   `LiveRegion` part (aria-live=polite), two `Messages` fields, a `live_message: String` Context
   field set in the relevant transitions, and an `Effect::Announce` (adapter surfaces
   `ctx.live_message`). Amend §1.3, §1.6, §1.7, §2 anatomy, §3.3, §4.1.

5. **Delimiter-on-type (issue requires an "add on comma" test).** §1.6's `InputChange` only sets
   `input_value`; only `Paste` splits on the delimiter. The §1.2 IME note ("delimiter detection
   MUST be suppressed during composition") implies delimiter detection happens during typing. Wire
   `InputChange` (when not composing and not editing) to split completed `delimiter`-separated
   segments into tags and keep the trailing remainder as `input_value`. Amend §1.6.

6. **Validation.** The issue lists an "on_validate callback" test, but the spec routes validation
   to the forms layer (§5 `PerTagValidator`) — core `Props` has no validate callback (the
   `// Change callbacks provided by the adapter layer` comment). Honor the spec: test the `invalid`
   prop / `aria-invalid` / ErrorMessage wiring instead of inventing a core callback.

## Files to create / modify

- **Create** `crates/ars-components/src/selection/tags_input/mod.rs` — the whole component:
  `State`, `Event`, `Context`, `Props` (+`#[derive(HasId)]` and builder methods matching the
  combobox/text_field convention), `BlurBehavior`, `Messages` (+`ComponentMessages`), `Effect`
  enum, `Part` (`#[derive(ComponentPart)] #[scope = "tags-input"]`), `Machine` (`impl
  ars_core::Machine`), `Api<'a>` + `impl ConnectApi`, effect helper fns, and inline
  `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (unit + snapshot).
- **Modify** `crates/ars-components/src/selection/mod.rs` — add `pub mod tags_input;` (alphabetical).
- **Modify** `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/selection.rs` — add
  `tags_input` to imports and a `tags_input_anatomy_matches_spec` test via `assert_anatomy`.
- **Modify** `crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/selection.rs` — add `tags_input`
  generators + an `#[ignore]` proptest of event-sequence invariants.
- **Modify** `spec/components/selection/tags-input.md` — land amendments 1-6 above.
- **Snapshots** auto-created under `crates/ars-components/src/selection/tags_input/snapshots/`.

## Canonical APIs to reuse (verified)

- Imports from `ars_core`: `AriaAttr, AttrMap, Bindable, ComponentIds, ComponentMessages,
  ComponentPart, ConnectApi, Env, HtmlAttr, KeyboardKey, Locale, MessageFn, PendingEffect,
  TransitionPlan, no_cleanup` (+ `ConnectApi as _`, `Service` in tests). `KeyboardEventData` from
  `ars_interactions`. Core trait/impl signatures confirmed in `crates/ars-core/src/lib.rs`.
- `Bindable<Vec<String>>` is valid (blanket `impl<T: Clone+PartialEq+Debug> BindableValue for T`).
- `TransitionPlan::context_only(f)` / `::to(state).apply(f)` / `.with_effect(PendingEffect::named(..))`.
- `ComponentIds::part(&str)` and `item(&str, &impl Display)` (accepts `usize`/`&usize`).
- `AttrMap::new()/set/set_bool`; all `HtmlAttr`/`AriaAttr` variants used in §1.7 exist.
- `MessageFn::new(closure)` and `MessageFn::static_str(&'static str)`.
- Effect convention: typed enum + `PendingEffect::named(Effect::X)`, adapter reads `ctx`
  (model: `crates/ars-components/src/input/pin_input/mod.rs`).
- Callback-style effects with props closures: `crates/ars-components/src/input/text_field/mod.rs`
  (`focus_change_effect`). LiveRegion part + on-demand text Api method:
  `crates/ars-components/src/selection/combobox/mod.rs` (`live_region_attrs`, `results_count_text`).

## Design summary

- **States** (§1.1): `Idle`, `Focused`, `EditingTag { index }`.
- **Context** (§1.3 + amendments): spec fields **plus** `editing_draft: String` and
  `live_message: String`. `value: Bindable<Vec<String>>` (Vec preserves insertion order; dup
  prevention in transition logic — tags are positional strings, not keyed `Collection` items, so
  parts key on `index: usize` exactly as the spec models).
- **Effect** enum: `FocusTag` (focus `ctx.focused_tag`), `FocusInput`, `FocusEditInput`
  (focus `ctx.editing_tag`), `Announce` (surface `ctx.live_message`).
- **Part** enum (13): `Root, Label, Control, Tag{index}, TagText{index}, TagDeleteTrigger{index},
  TagEdit{index}, Input, ClearTrigger, HiddenInput, Description, ErrorMessage, LiveRegion`.
- **Transitions**: implement §1.6 exactly except the reconciliations — disabled/readonly guards;
  `AddTag` (trim, max, dup); `RemoveTag`/`RemoveTagAtIndex` (set `live_message` via
  `removed_announcement`, refocus adjacent tag via `FocusTag`, or `FocusInput`+`Announce` when
  empty); `EditTag`/`CommitEdit`/`CancelEdit` (editable/disabled/readonly guard, `editing_draft`);
  `Focus`/`Blur` (+ `EditingTag` blur commit + `BlurBehavior`); `InputChange` (delimiter-on-type
  split when not composing/editing; else `editing_draft`); `Paste`; `ClearAll`; `FocusPrevTag`/
  `FocusNextTag`; `CompositionStart`/`End`; `AddTag` blocked by max → `Announce` max-reached.
- **Api / connect** (§1.7): one method per part returning `AttrMap` exactly per §1.6 (grid pattern:
  `Control` role=grid, `Tag` role=row, `TagText`/`TagDeleteTrigger` role=gridcell), plus
  `count_text()`, `live_region_attrs()`, an announcement-text accessor, deduped event handlers, and
  `impl ConnectApi { fn part_attrs }`.

## Test plan (TDD — write tests first, per issue "Tests to add first")

Inline unit tests in `mod.rs` (drive via `Service::<Machine>::new(props, &Env::default(),
&Messages::default())`, `service.send(event)`, `service.connect(&send)`):
add-on-Enter; add-on-comma (delimiter-on-type); remove via delete button (`RemoveTag`) and via
Backspace (empty input → focus last; focused tag → remove); arrow-key tag navigation; duplicate
prevention; max-tags limit (+ max-reached announcement); `invalid` state attrs + ErrorMessage;
inline edit (`EditTag`/`CommitEdit`/`CancelEdit` + `editing_draft`); paste split; clear-all; IME
suppression (Enter/delimiter no-op while composing); blur behavior (`Add`/`Clear`); removal
announcement live region.

Snapshot tests (`insta::assert_snapshot!` over a `snapshot_attrs(&attrs)` Debug helper, matching
the checkbox/combobox pattern): every anatomy part **and** each output-affecting state/prop/context
branch — root (default/disabled/readonly/invalid/focused), control (±max), tag
(focused/editing/disabled/focus-visible), tag_text, tag_delete_trigger (±disabled), tag_edit
(editing/hidden), input (placeholder/disabled/readonly/required/max_length/at-max/invalid-describedby),
clear_trigger (±disabled), hidden_input (name/required/disabled), description, error_message
(visible/hidden), live_region. Commit `.snap` fixtures; must pass
`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject`. Attach `snapshot-reviewed` label to the PR.

Spec-conformance: `tags_input_anatomy_matches_spec` enumerating all 13 parts in declared order.

Proptest: `arb_*` props/events + an `#[ignore]` sequence test asserting invariants — no duplicates
when `!allow_duplicates`, `len <= max`, `focused_tag`/`editing_tag` in range, `hidden_input` value
== `value.join(delimiter)`, stable id.

## Verification

1. `cargo test -p ars-components tags_input` (unit + snapshot) and
   `cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject` — review/accept new `.snap` files.
2. `cargo test -p ars-components --test spec_conformance tags_input_anatomy_matches_spec`.
3. `cargo test -p ars-components --test proptest_state_machines` (sanity; `#[ignore]` proptest is
   nightly-driven).
4. Run the mandatory `post-implementation-audit` skill (all three phases — spec/impl drift,
   iterative "anything missing?", test coverage); fix every finding in this PR.
5. After user approval: `cargo xci-fast` (fmt, clippy, unit/integration/adapter, snapshot-count,
   native coverage ≥ 90/90 for `ars-components`) — fix failures before push.
6. Open PR targeting `main` with `Closes #242`; add `snapshot-reviewed` label; then run the
   `waiting-for-codex-review` skill loop until Codex 👍.

## Out of scope

Leptos/Dioxus adapter components, adapter tests, widgets examples, E2E fixtures (those are a
separate adapter task per `docs/implementation/adapter-component-delivery.md`). Mutation testing is
nightly-driven, not a delivery gate.

content.content:
1	//! TextField component state machine and connect API.
2	//!
3	//! This module implements the framework-agnostic `TextField` machine defined in
4	//! `spec/components/input/text-field.md`. The native input element is the form
5	//! participant; no hidden input is emitted for this component.
6	
7	use alloc::{
8	    string::{String, ToString},
9	    vec::Vec,
10	};
11	use core::fmt::{self, Debug, Display};
12	
13	use ars_core::{
14	    AriaAttr, AttrMap, Bindable, Callback, ComponentIds, ComponentMessages, ComponentPart,
15	    ConnectApi, Direction, Env, HtmlAttr, InputMode, KeyboardKey, Locale, MessageFn, PendingEffect,
16	    TransitionPlan, no_cleanup,
17	};
18	use ars_interactions::KeyboardEventData;
19	
20	/// The states for the `TextField` component.
21	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
22	pub enum State {
23	    /// The component is in an idle state.
24	    Idle,
25	
26	    /// The component is in a focused state.
27	    Focused,
28	}
29	
30	impl Display for State {
31	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
32	        f.write_str(match self {
33	            Self::Idle => "idle",
34	            Self::Focused => "focused",
35	        })
36	    }
37	}
38	
39	/// The events for the `TextField` component.
40	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
41	pub enum Event {
42	    /// The component received a focus event.
43	    Focus {
44	        /// True if the focus was initiated by a keyboard.
45	        is_keyboard: bool,
46	    },
47	
48	    /// The component received a blur event.
49	    Blur,
50	
51	    /// The component received a change event.
52	    Change(String),
53	
54	    /// The component received a clear event.
55	    Clear,
56	
57	    /// The component received a set invalid event.
58	    SetInvalid(bool),
59	
60	    /// IME composition started.

160	    pub autocomplete: Option<String>,
161	
162	    /// The name of the component.
163	    pub name: Option<String>,
164	
165	    /// True while an IME composition session is active.
166	    pub is_composing: bool,
167	
168	    /// Whether a Description part is rendered.
169	    pub has_description: bool,
170	
171	    /// Text direction for RTL support.
172	    pub dir: Direction,
173	
174	    /// Mobile on-screen keyboard layout hint.
175	    pub input_mode: Option<InputMode>,
176	
177	    /// Resolved locale for i18n.
178	    pub locale: Locale,
179	
180	    /// Resolved translatable messages.
181	    pub messages: Messages,
182	
183	    /// Component IDs for part identification.
184	    pub ids: ComponentIds,
185	}
186	
187	/// The props for the `TextField` component.
188	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, ars_core::HasId)]
189	pub struct Props {
190	    /// Adapter-provided base ID for the text field root.
191	    pub id: String,
192	
193	    /// Controlled value. When `Some`, component is controlled.
194	    pub value: Option<String>,
195	
196	    /// Default value for uncontrolled mode.
197	    pub default_value: String,
198	
199	    /// Whether the component is disabled.
200	    pub disabled: bool,
201	
202	    /// Whether the component is readonly.
203	    pub readonly: bool,
204	
205	    /// Whether the component is invalid.
206	    pub invalid: bool,
207	
208	    /// Whether the component is required.
209	    pub required: bool,
210	
211	    /// The placeholder of the component.
212	    pub placeholder: Option<String>,
213	
214	    /// The input type of the component.
215	    pub input_type: InputType,
216	
217	    /// The maximum length of the component.
218	    pub max_length: Option<u32>,
219	
220	    /// The minimum length of the component.
221	    pub min_length: Option<u32>,
222	
223	    /// The pattern of the component.
224	    pub pattern: Option<String>,
225	
226	    /// The autocomplete of the component.
227	    pub autocomplete: Option<String>,
228	
229	    /// The name of the component.
230	    pub name: Option<String>,
231	
232	    /// The ID of the form element the input is associated with.
233	    pub form: Option<String>,
234	
235	    /// Whether the component is clearable.
236	    pub clearable: bool,
237	
238	    /// The direction of the component.
239	    pub dir: Direction,
240	
241	    /// Hint for the virtual keyboard type on mobile devices.
242	    pub input_mode: Option<InputMode>,
243	
244	    /// Convenience callback fired with `true` on Focus and `false` on Blur.
245	    pub on_focus_change: Option<Callback<dyn Fn(bool) + Send + Sync>>,
246	
247	    /// Callback fired when user interaction requests a value change.
248	    pub on_value_change: Option<Callback<dyn Fn(String) + Send + Sync>>,
249	}
250	
251	impl Default for Props {
252	    fn default() -> Self {
253	        Self {
254	            id: String::new(),
255	            value: None,
256	            default_value: String::new(),
257	            disabled: false,
258	            readonly: false,
259	            invalid: false,
260	            required: false,
261	            placeholder: None,
262	            input_type: InputType::Text,
263	            max_length: None,
264	            min_length: None,
265	            pattern: None,
266	            autocomplete: None,
267	            name: None,
268	            form: None,
269	            clearable: false,
270	            dir: Direction::Ltr,
271	            input_mode: None,
272	            on_focus_change: None,
273	            on_value_change: None,
274	        }
275	    }
276	}
277	
278	impl Props {
279	    /// Returns a fresh [`Props`] with every field at its [`Default`] value.
